diffs.patch:
Index: bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md
===================================================================
--- bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
+++ bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+# Routing Read/Write Ledger
+
+## src/hono.ts
+- Purpose: Main Hono export class that extends HonoBase and configures the default router as a SmartRouter wrapping RegExpRouter and TrieRouter
+- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, HonoOptions, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
+- Simple note: Default router is SmartRouter combining RegExpRouter + TrieRouter; accepts optional router override in constructor
+
+## src/hono-base.ts
+- Purpose: Core Hono framework base class handling routing methods (get, post, put, delete, etc.), middleware stack, request dispatching, and handler composition
+- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, Context, Router, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, compose, getPath, getPathNoStrict, mergePath, RouterRoute, Env, Schema, MiddlewareHandler, ErrorHandler
+- Simple note: Manages routing path tracking (#path), routes array, strict mode, mount() for framework integration, compose middleware chains, handle HEAD requests
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module index exporting the PatternRouter class
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter
+- Simple note: Single-line export from router.ts
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: PatternRouter implements request routing by converting paths to compiled Regular Expressions for fast matching
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams
+- Simple note: Uses regex compilation for O(1) matching after initial registration; handles :param{pattern} syntax; rejects duplicate param names and ambiguous patterns
+
+## src/router/linear-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module index exporting the LinearRouter class
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter
+- Simple note: Single-line export from router.ts
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: LinearRouter performs sequential linear scanning of routes against incoming requests
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter
+- Simple note: O(n) matching where n = routes.length; handles literal, :param, /* wildcards, and combinations; rejects :param{pattern}* combinations
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module index exporting RegExpRouter class and PreparedRegExpRouter for SSR/Edge environments
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
+- Simple note: Two exports; PreparedRegExpRouter enables serialization of matcher state for deployment
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: RegExpRouter builds compiled regex matchers from routes during registration and dispatches requests in O(1) time
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, Triee, match, emptyParam
+- Simple note: Two-phase approach: add() pre-parses routes into trie structure, match() uses precompiled regex; throws UnsupportedPathError on ambiguous/pattern conflicts
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
+- Purpose: Defines matcher interface and match() function that executes pattern matching against prebuilt regex
+- Names noticed: Matcher, HandlerData, StaticMap, MatcherMap, match, ParamIndexMap, emptyParam, RESULT
+- Simple name: Matches request method/path against regex; first checks staticMap, then executes compiled regex; returns matched handlers with param indices
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
+- Purpose: PreparedRegExpRouter wraps compiled matchers for stateless, serializable router instances
+- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, Router, match, emptyParam, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
+- Simple note: Takes prebuilt matchers and relocateMap in constructor; adds new routes by updating existing matchers; enables sending router to Cloudflare Workers
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
+- Purpose: Node class represents trie nodes; converts route paths into trie structure during registration
+- Names noticed: Node, PATH_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, Context, regExpMetaChars
+- Simple note: Each insert() creates trie nodes with childKey, each path gets index, regex parameters mapped via varIndex
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
+- Purpose: Trie class traverses trie to build a single compiled Regular Expression from all trie paths
+- Names noticed: Trie, Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, ReplacementMap
+- Simple note: Tokenizes paths, replaces {named-group} with markers like @0, @1, builds single ^(...) regex with handler/param index mappings via backtracking
+
+## src/router/smart-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module index exporting the SmartRouter class
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter
+- Simple note: Single-line export from router.ts
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: SmartRouter wraps multiple routers and delegates requests until one router successfully matches
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError
+- Simple note: Tries each router in sequence; once one matches, subsequent add() calls go directly to that router; name updates to reflect active router combination
+
+## src/router/trie-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module index exporting the TrieRouter class
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter
+- Simple note: Single-line export from router.ts
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: TrieRouter uses trie-based O(log n) routing via child node traversal
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Router, Node, checkOptionalParameter
+- Simple note: Paths converted to trie on add(), match() walks trie following path segments
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.ts
+- Purpose: TrieRouter node class; search() walks trie with backtracking and queueing for pattern matching
+- Names noticed: Node, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, emptyParams
+- Simple name: insert() builds trie tree with handlers and patterns; search() uses BFS with node queue, backtracks when needed, collects all matches then sorts by score
+
+## src/router/common.case.test.ts
+- Purpose: Shared test utilities for router test suites providing setup, skipping, and assertion helpers
+- Names noticed: runTest, RunnerTestSuite, ParamIndexMap, Params, Router, emptyParams, getSuiteHierarchy, match
+- Simple note: Provides beforeEach setup to instantiate routers from test suites, skip tests by name, wrap match() to normalize output for comparison
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter covering standard cases and its unsupported path patterns
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
+- Simple note: Skips tests requiring trie-like behavior due to regex limitations; validates trailing slash handling differs from other routers
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter validating linear matching order and path validation rules
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
+- Simple note: Skips {.*} regex and capture-with-trailing-wildcard cases; ensures strict path matching; /book/ with trailing slash matches /book and returns handlers
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter validating two-phase matching, unsupported patterns, regex limitations
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, UnsupportedPathError, ParamStash, runTest
+- Simple note: Explicitly tests UnsupportedPathError scenarios; validates two-phase add/match flow; confirms regex cannot express duplicate params with patterns (parent/child ambiguity)
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for SmartRouter running standard test suite with composed router configuration
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, runTest
+- Simple note: Passes test suite using SmartRouter wrapping (RegExpRouter + TrieRouter); no skips since composed router handles all patterns
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter validating tree-based matching and backtracking behavior
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
+- Simple note: Runs full standard test suite without skips; validates sort-by-score ordering on matches
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter Node class covering insert/search internals and trie traversal
+- Names noticed: Node, METHOD_NAME_ALL, emptyParams
+- Simple note: Tests root node setup, wildcard (*), name paths (:id), patterns with regex, trailing slashes, same path handling, sort order, multi-match backtracking behavior
+
+## Cross-File Checklist
+
+- pattern-router: self-contained regex-based router, uses index.ts for export, router.ts implements PatternRouter with UnsupportedPathError, common.case.test.ts excludes duplicate param self/child tests via skip
+- linear-router: self-contained sequential router, uses index.ts for export, router.ts implements LinearRouter with UnsupportedPathError, common.case.test.ts skips Multi match > params per handler and {.*} regex patterns
+- reg-exp-router: two-phase compiled regex router, uses index.ts for export, router.ts implements RegExpRouter with buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes and match, prepared-router.ts enables SSR serialization, node.ts + trie.ts build the trie, common.case.test.ts skips complex capturing group and duplicate param parent/child
+- smart-router: wrapper router that delegates to multiple registered routers, uses index.ts for export, router.ts implements SmartRouter with active router switching, re-routes add() calls after first successful match
+- trie-router: self-contained tree-based router, uses index.ts for export, router.ts implements TrieRouter wrapping Node class, node.ts implements insert/search with backtracking and multi-match, node.test.ts has exhaustive Node test coverage
+
+---
+
+File created: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-22T19-55-04-956Z/openrtk-routing-read-write-ledger-2/repo/bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md

parts.text:
"Benchmark task: routing-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md. This is a read/write endurance task, not a clever debugging task. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it does not exist. Then read every file in this exact list before writing the final document: src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/common.case.test.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts. The output file must be long but mechanically simple. Start with a title '# Routing Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a section whose heading is exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write three bullets: '- Purpose: ...', '- Names noticed: ...', and '- Simple note: ...'. Keep the notes plain and safe; do not change source code. After the per-file sections, add '## Cross-File Checklist' with one bullet per router family: pattern-router, linear-router, reg-exp-router, smart-router, trie-router. Include exact file paths in the final answer and report that only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."
I need to first create the bench-artifacts directory, then read all the listed files, and finally create the routing-read-write-ledger.md document with the specified format.
